  The paper does not provide explicit details on the parameter count (p) of the models used or the training set size (N). However, the context discusses the integration of AI and machine learning (ML) models with RT-PCR, mentioning that these models helped in overcoming elongated analysis time with quick responses. The use of ML models in this context typically involves a manageable number of parameters relative to the data volume, suggesting that the parameter count is likely not larger than the training set size.
